summary: DressVibe backend — 'Aggiungi accessori' feature + GET /api/generations 500 fix tested end-to-end against the public preview URL (https://outfit-gen-11.preview.emergentagent.com) using the canonical bearer 'test_session_screen' → user_demo01. All 10 pytest cases pass (100%). Critical assertion of the brief — GET /api/generations returns 200 (not 500) — verified. POST /api/generations works with and without accessories, the `accessories` field is correctly stripped from the persisted params doc, invalid categories fall back to 'altro' instead of erroring, and Pydantic validation enforces required fields (422 on missing image_base64 or category). Unit tests on the new helper _compose_accessories_suffix confirm correct reference-index numbering after garment refs.

minor.issue: The aggregation $project for `thumbnail` uses `$ifNull` over `$arrayElemAt` on possibly-missing arrays — when a generation has `image_count == 0` (e.g. status='failed' with no images), MongoDB silently omits the `thumbnail` key entirely from the projected document instead of returning `thumbnail: null`. Frontend must therefore treat the field as optional. To always emit the key, wrap with an outer `$ifNull` returning null explicitly, e.g. `{$ifNull: [<existing>, null]}` AND make the elements `$ifNull`-safe.

action_items: (Optional) Make the `thumbnail` field always present in the GET /api/generations response (even when null) so the frontend doesn't have to check for both 'absent' and 'null'.
(Optional polish) Same observation as iteration_1: persisted params field strip is correct, but persisted doc still holds `model_preset_id`/heavy fields — out of scope here.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 4095 lines — strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth/, garments/, generations/, studio/, social/) as the next refactor. File-size is well past the 700-line guideline.

AccessoryItem has no enum validation on `category`. This is intentional per the brief (falls back to 'altro' instruction), but it does mean a client typo silently degrades the prompt quality. Consider using `Literal[...]` with `Field(..., examples=[...])` and a server-side warning log when the value is outside the curated set.

Accessories are correctly stripped from `params` (`payload.dict(exclude={'garment_ids','title','accessories'})`) so the DB does not grow with disposable base64 — verified end-to-end via the GET detail check.

The aggregation pipeline for the list endpoint correctly projects BEFORE `$sort`, avoiding the MongoDB Atlas 32 MB in-memory-sort budget that caused the original 500. The composite (user_id, created_at) index mentioned in the brief should keep the pipeline cheap as the collection grows.
`_compose_accessories_suffix` is well-commented; the 1-based ref-index math (num_garments + i) is correct and reproducible from the dispatcher.
MongoDB `_id` continues to be excluded from every response (confirmed by inspecting generation detail/list bodies in the test run).
seed_data_creation: An autouse session fixture in /app/backend/tests/test_accessories.py idempotently upserts (a) user_id=user_demo01 in `users` and (b) the bearer token `test_session_screen` in `user_sessions` with a 7-day expiry. The fixture leaves these rows in place (no teardown) so the demo session keeps working across iterations. If a demo garment is absent it creates one TEST_ACC_Garment, and every TEST_ACC_* generation is deleted at the end of each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_accessories.py for accessories + list-endpoint regression. It seeds user_demo01 + bearer 'test_session_screen' automatically via Motor. Run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_accessories.py -v`. Each generation POST hits the real Gemini Nano Banana (~15-20s each, 3 calls in this suite, ~50s total). If the Gemini free tier rate-limits with 429, the tests degrade gracefully (200 OR 429 both accepted, status='failed'/'rate_limited' tolerated). The original 500 from QueryExceededMemoryLimitNoDiskUseAllowed is now fixed — confirmed by the test_list_returns_200_not_500 case and the 'GET /api/generations HTTP/1.1 200 OK' lines in /var/log/supervisor/backend.out.log during the run. The iteration_1 suite (/app/backend/tests/test_dressvibe_api.py) still applies for non-accessories regressions and uses a different ephemeral TEST_ user.
rca_of_issue: Original 500 on GET /api/generations: Mongo `$sort` was applied to documents that still contained the full base64 `images` array (often 500 KB–2 MB each). Atlas free-tier rejects in-memory sort > 32 MB with QueryExceededMemoryLimitNoDiskUseAllowed. The fix moves the sort AFTER a `$project` stage that keeps only id/title/status/created_at + a computed image_count and the first thumb/image (typically 6–15 KB), making the working set ≤ 100 KB per request. Verified in this run: GET /api/generations returns 200 with 84+ items and zero `images` key leakage.
